title: Presigned Downloads, CloudFront Signatures, Cookies, and Revocation · Language Lens: S3 Presigned Download
summary: A product places long-lived S3 presigned URLs in logs, uses one shared CloudFront key, signs with legacy assumptions, and promises immediate revocation after links escape. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the s3 presigned download increment in time-bounded signed-delivery decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Presign the exact version, response metadata, method, region, credential scope, and shortest useful expiry while treating the URL as a bearer secret.
Prove new writes, old reads, role rotation, key disable recovery, compromised-session revocation limits, and residual cached or signed access.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict S3 Presigned Download · Theory S3 Presigned Download
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 3006 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound signed access by time, resource, key group, and explicit SHA-256 review; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
